opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My skin has turned completely yellow, the itching is driving me insane, and I've lost 10 kilograms without even trying.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last couple of months I've dropped a lot of weight and gone right off my food, and in the last few weeks my skin and the whites of my eyes have gone deep yellow with unbearable itching. My urine has gone dark like tea and my stools have turned pale.

symptom_narrative.onset: the yellowing and itching started about 3 weeks ago; the weight loss and loss of appetite have been going on for about 2 months
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly 3 weeks of yellow skin and itching, 2 months of weight loss
symptom_narrative.character: a vague, deep, dull ache in the upper middle of my belly that seems to bore straight through to my back, plus relentless itching all over and yellow skin and eyes
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is unbearable and stops me sleeping, and I feel completely wiped out and weak
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: upper middle of the belly going through to the back; the yellow is all over my skin and in the whites of my eyes
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and steadily getting worse, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — the anti-itch creams barely touch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: dark tea-coloured urine, pale greasy stools that are hard to flush, no appetite at all, and feeling exhausted; there is no pain when the yellow comes on
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this has ever happened before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: moving around slowly, exhausted all the time, and the itching keeps me awake at night

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: I was told I'd become diabetic about 4 months ago, which was a surprise — I'm not overweight and there's no diabetes in my family

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol, which I've been taking for the dull back ache

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no diabetes and, as far as I know, no cancer in the family

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with spouse, who is present and worried about how much weight has come off

objective_data_offered.value: deep yellow discolouration of the skin and the whites of both eyes, visible on video
skin covered in scratch marks from constant itching
about 62 kg on the home scales, down roughly 10 kg over 2 months
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-weighed on a home scale; the 10 kg figure is the patient's estimate of the loss

objective_data_offered.value: urine has gone dark, like strong tea
stools have turned pale, greasy and hard to flush
checked with a home thermometer and it read normal, around 36.6
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home thermometer reading; the patient reports no fevers or shivering
